name: data-freshness-stamp
title: Data Freshness Stamp
description: How old a reading is, and whether its source is still reporting — fresh, stale, or disconnected.

props.name: age
props.description: How old, in the caller's words: "4 min ago", "09:12", "6 h ago".

props.name: status
props.description: `disconnected` says the source went silent — never the same claim as "unchanged".

props.name: label
props.description: What the age refers to — "Last synced", "Last charted". Read out to assistive tech.

props.name: detail
props.description: Why it is stale or disconnected. Renders in a tooltip.

clinicalNotes: A chart quietly showing yesterday's values looks exactly like one showing this morning's, so the age of the data is part of the reading rather than chrome. The `disconnected` state exists because a monitor that has stopped reporting and a patient whose values have stopped changing produce the same flat line — the UI must distinguish them, and a caller should prefer `disconnected` over `stale` whenever the source itself is known to be silent. The component reports the age it is given; it does not decide when a reading becomes stale, because that threshold is clinical (a blood pressure from four hours ago is routine on a ward and unacceptable in theatre).
notes: Sits beside charts, flowsheet headers, and vitals panels. Composes badge + tooltip.
